mnemonics: D.E.C.O.D.E.R: Decoders Enable Correct Outputs by Decoding Encoded Representations
keyPoints: A decoder is a combinational circuit that converts binary information from n inputs to a maximum of 2^n unique outputs
Only one output is active (typically high) for any given input combination, representing the decoded address
Decoders are fundamental building blocks for memory address selection and control logic
commonMistakes: Confusing decoders (many outputs) with encoders (many inputs), which perform the inverse function
Forgetting that decoder outputs are mutually exclusive - only one can be active at any time
Misunderstanding active-high vs. active-low output configurations and their impact on circuit design
